raw_text:
Approved — the mapping is faithful, priorities are right, and the assignments maximize leverage.

One adjustment (important)

Even though you created them as independent, enforce an execution order constraint informally so you don’t chase downstream noise:

Run order for merges/tests

bd-2hz (env var normalization) — removes config flake early

bd-3d7 (audit migration idempotency) — clears 12 failures and stabilizes fleet runs

bd-25z (/api/version auth policy) — clears ops/test gating issues

bd-29g (envelope + oracle invariants) — only meaningful once infra/config/auth are stable

bd-3d4 (GL UUID test fix) — quick cleanup

bd-kaw / bd-3bi (AR + Subscriptions drift) — last, because they may require domain decisions

If you can, treat bd-29g as “merge last” even if it finishes earlier.

Stop-the-line rules

No loosening invariants to make tests pass (especially in bd-29g).

For bd-3d7, do not use destructive DROP patterns that can delete audit data.

For bd-25z, only exempt /api/version and /api/ready — nothing else.

What I want back

After these land, run exactly:

cargo test -p e2e-tests --no-fail-fast -- --nocapture

Paste the final summary line: PASS / FAIL / IGNORED / TOTAL plus any remaining ignored list.

Proceed.
